Watanabe Shikō

3 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Flowers and Trees of the Four Seasons (right screen)
by Watanabe Shikō
Japan, Edo period (1615–1868) · early to mid-1700s · Six-panel folding screen; ink, color, gold, silver, and gilding on paper
Eight Views of the Xiao and Xiang Rivers
by Watanabe Shikō
Japan, Edo period (1615–1868) · 1700s · Six-panel folding screen, ink on paper
Flowers and Trees of the Four Seasons
by Watanabe Shikō
Edo Japan · Watanabe Shikō · early 18th c.
A six-panel folding screen of seasonal trees and flowers — cherry, maple, pine — painted in the refined Rinpa manner that came down from Sōtatsu and Kōrin into the mid Edo period.
←Kano Shigenobu Shitao→
